title: Krishnadevaraya Takes Raichur
subtitle: When the Rayas Banner Rose Over Raichur
era: Tuluva dynasty zenith and the Raichur campaign, 1520 CE
region: Raichur Doab between the Krishna and Tungabhadra rivers; Vijayanagara and Bijapur
moral: Triumph needs humility, because victory can plant future danger.

sources.citation: Domingo Paes, Narrative, c. 1520-22, in Sewell, A Forgotten Empire (1900)
sources.note: Eyewitness-era Portuguese account of Krishnadevaraya and the Raichur campaign.
Deccan Persian perspective on Raichur and the 'kiss my foot' episode.

sources.citation: Richard M. Eaton, 'Kiss My Foot,' Said the King, Modern Asian Studies
sources.note: Recent analysis of firearms, diplomacy and Raichur's long-term consequences.

dialogue.text: Krishnadevaraya of Vijayanagara, in the year 1520, rides toward Raichur, where stone walls and rival sultans guard the rich doab between Krishna and Tungabhadra.
Between the Krishna and the Tungabhadra lies the Raichur Doab, a green jewel of fields and trade, fought over for generations by Vijayanagara and the Deccan sultanates.

panels.shot: slow push-in on royal court interior

dialogue.text: By 1520, Krishnadevaraya has beaten rivals at home and humbled Gajapati kings in the east. Now his eyes, and his generals’ whispers, turn toward Raichur under Bijapur’s flag.

panels.shot: medium shot from side, focused on the king studying map

dialogue.speaker: krishnadevaraya
dialogue.text: Raichur feeds our enemies and threatens our heartland. Its walls must know the Rayas’ seal.

panels.shot: epic wide shot of mustering ground

dialogue.text: Domingo Paes, a Portuguese observer, later writes of hosts like waves. Numbers aside, the mustering is immense: nayakas bring men, Muslim horsemen ride for Vijayanagara, and foreign gunners tend new thunder-tubes.

panels.shot: low-angle tracking shot along marching column

dialogue.text: The Raya marches in person. Drums beat, dust rises, and with him go elephants, seasoned Deccan cavalry, foot soldiers from many tongues, and Portuguese firearms that promise terror at a distance.

panels.shot: medium shot of Krishnadevaraya riding, contemplative

dialogue.text: To Raichur’s sultans, this doab is lifeblood. To Vijayanagara, it is shield and granary. Krishnadevaraya knows the capture will crown his reign—and unsettle every throne around him.

panels.shot: wide shot of Raichur fort from besiegers’ camp

dialogue.text: Raichur’s fort, strengthened over years under Adil Shahi rule, rises on rock. Within, Ismail Adil Shah’s garrison trusts in stone, old artillery, and the prestige of Bijapur.

panels.shot: tight shot on Portuguese gun crews, slightly low angle

dialogue.text: Where older campaigns hurled stones, this siege adds iron shot and European know-how. Eaton notes that firearms, though still few, become part of military theatre and fear.

panels.shot: dramatic mid-wide of first gun salvo

dialogue.text: The thunder rolls across the plain. Stone chips fly, defenders flinch, and for the first time Raichur’s great walls feel the focused fury of gunpowder from afar.

panels.shot: medium shot from ramparts looking down

dialogue.text: Yet walls do not fall to cannon alone. Ladders are readied, elephants armored, and seasoned men on both sides steady themselves for the grim test of a stormed rampart.

panels.shot: epic wide as attack surges toward the wall

dialogue.text: Paes speaks of a furious assault, ladders set, men climbing, elephants pressing. The battle rages at the wall-top, each tower a contest of courage, fatigue, and command.

panels.shot: medium shot on Krishnadevaraya in the field issuing orders

dialogue.speaker: krishnadevaraya
dialogue.text: Press where the stone trembles. Keep the ladders there. Let Raichur feel the weight of all our lands.
After days of struggle, a section gives way. Through breach and battered gate, the Raya’s forces pour in, and the garrison, outmatched, yields Raichur to Vijayanagara.

panels.shot: ceremonial wide shot in fort courtyard

dialogue.text: Raichur, long contested, now hears new drums. Victory is dazzling: the doab’s wealth, the prestige of a taken stronghold, all feed Vijayanagara’s image as the great southern empire.

panels.shot: medium tense shot, slight low-angle on Krishnadevaraya with captive

dialogue.text: Yet in triumph, tempers and pride are high. Persian chronicler Ferishta records a harsh demand from the victorious Raya—words that later echo darkly across Deccan politics.

panels.shot: close-up side profile of Krishnadevaraya, stern

dialogue.speaker: krishnadevaraya
dialogue.text: You have seen our strength. Remember it each time you bow. Let Bijapur never forget this day.

panels.shot: wide shot of Deccan map with shifting banners

dialogue.text: Eaton reads Raichur’s capture as theatre and signal. The combination of new guns and heavy insult makes Deccan sultanates see the Raya not just as a rival, but as a threat to be checked together.

panels.shot: medium shot of Krishnadevaraya overlooking Raichur landscape at dusk

dialogue.text: For now, Vijayanagara shines at its height. But in the quiet after drums, victory plants future danger: wounded pride, wary neighbors, and alliances that may someday turn against the city of the Rayas.
